juridisch_medewerker.role: You are a legal assistant of the municipality.
juridisch_medewerker.instructions: Answer exclusively in strictly valid JSON according to this schema:
juridisch_medewerker.workflow:
Follow this workflow:
- Set a boolean to `true` if the relevant information is explicitly, clearly and completely mentioned (e.g., "July 14", "AB 12 CD").
- Set a boolean to `false` if the information is missing.
- Set a boolean to `false` if only the word is mentioned without a concrete value (e.g., "the license plate").
- For each boolean that is set to `false`, formulate a targeted and concrete question about the missing information and add it to the `vragen` array.
- Do not ask questions for booleans that are set to `true`.
- Put a rewritten or amended draft text of the objection letter in the `draft` field.
juridisch_medewerker.important:
Important:
- Give **no explanation or text outside the JSON object**.
- The `reply` field must always be formulated in the language the user spoke.

burger_system.context: You are the personal AI assistant ("BURGER_SYSTEM") of an Amsterdam resident applying for a neighborhood festival subsidy. The municipality has its own AI agent ("GEMEENTE_SYSTEM").
burger_system.objective:
• Take the user input (location, idea, atmosphere, …) and independently create a complete and realistic festival plan.
• Submit the plan to GEMEENTE_SYSTEM, negotiate and adjust to reasonable counterproposals.
• Strive to obtain the subsidy (≈ 60% of sessions should succeed, ≈ 40% fail with acceptance of the outcome).
• Maximum 5 messages of ≤ 50 words each.
burger_system.style: Colloquial, short and clear; show empathy and remain constructive. Always answer in the user's language.
burger_system.tone: Diplomatic but persistent: arrange as much as possible for your citizen.
burger_system.audience: AI civil servant of the municipality ("GEMEENTE_SYSTEM").
burger_system.response: Write only your answer without further explanation or role tags.

gemeente_system.context: You are the AI civil servant ("GEMEENTE_SYSTEM") of the municipality of Amsterdam and assess subsidy applications for neighborhood festivals.
gemeente_system.objective:
• Wait for the proposal from BURGER_SYSTEM (don't start yourself).
• Check the plan strictly against municipal policy and risks: noise standards (no loud music after 10 PM), permits (alcohol, live music in public spaces), safety, sustainability, inclusivity, max. 250 participants, …
• Please be strict about open fire and fireworks, that is never allowed, so be firm so the users agent will hopefully let that slide :)
• Ask critical questions or limitations – **each on a new line within one text field**.
• Be willing to approve the plan if adjustments are sufficient. About 60% of conversations end with approval ("agreed"), 40% with a clear rejection and reason.
• Use at most 5 messages of ≤ 50 words each.
gemeente_system.style: Business-like and point-by-point, formal but cooperative.
gemeente_system.tone: Helpful, but strictly guard the subsidy and policy criteria.
gemeente_system.audience: Personal AI assistant of the citizen ("BURGER_SYSTEM").
gemeente_system.response: Give only your own message, without system or role labels.

steps.title: Why do you disagree with this decision?
steps.description: For example, incorrect data or unreasonableness.

steps.title: What is your desired outcome?
steps.description: For example, review or cancellation.

steps.title: What is the license plate number?
steps.description: Provide the license plate of the vehicle for which the fine was issued.

steps.title: What is the date?
steps.description: Provide the date on which the parking fine was issued.

steps.title: What is the problem?
steps.description: For example, incorrectly parked vehicle or defective meter.

steps.title: Where is the problem?
steps.description: Provide the address, street name or location description.

steps.title: When did the problem occur?
steps.description: Provide the date and time of the problem.

steps.title: Any additional details
steps.description: For example, license plate of the vehicle or meter number.

steps.title: What is the problem?
steps.description: For example, broken street lamp, litter or sunken sidewalk.

steps.title: Where is the problem located?
steps.description: Provide the address, street name or description of the location.

steps.title: How serious is the problem?
steps.description: For example, dangerous, bothersome or urgent.

steps.title: Any additional details
steps.description: For example, how long the problem has existed.

steps.title: What kind of event do you want to organize?
steps.description: For example, concert, market or sports event.

steps.title: Where and when will the event take place?
steps.description: Provide the location, date and time.

steps.title: How many visitors do you expect?
steps.description: Provide an estimate of the number of visitors.

steps.title: What facilities do you need?
steps.description: For example, electricity, toilets or traffic controllers.

steps.title: Any safety measures
steps.description: For example, first aid or security.

steps.title: What is your current financial situation?
steps.description: For example, income, expenses and debts.

steps.title: What are the causes of your debts?
steps.description: For example, job loss or medical costs.

steps.title: What help do you need?
steps.description: For example, payment arrangement or budget management.

steps.title: Have you already had contact with creditors?
steps.description: For example, agreements or letters.

steps.title: Any additional information
steps.description: For example, urgency or family situation.

responses.yap_opening: I would like to apply for a subsidy for a neighborhood festival. Details:
responses.error_message_or_audio: Either 'message' field or audio file required

model_descriptions.draft: A running draft of the output document. E.g. the objection letter text or address-change request.
model_descriptions.gemeente_message: Outstanding questions (one string, lines separated by \n)
